mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending corporate earnings contraction, causing equity valuations to compress and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment trigger, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in industrial production creates a stagflationary environment that erodes real margins and suppresses equity multiples, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when both inflation accelerates and industrial output contracts, or the joint signal fails to outperform a CPI-only baseline in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts and expanding retail sales credit indicate strengthening consumer demand and business investment, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined housing and retail sales signal predicts negative returns during confirmed recovery phases, or forward returns do not exceed the historical median by at least 1.5 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to trigger multiple compression in growth-oriented equities, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when the funds rate crosses 3.5%, or the signal flips to positive predictive power during high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and robust aggregate supply, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth consistently coincides with negative equity returns, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC never exceeds 0.05 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payrolls growth and a persistently low unemployment rate indicates an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, ultimately depressing equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when payroll growth exceeds 150k and unemployment stays below 4%, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential decline in real GDP growth signals weakening aggregate demand and impending earnings revisions downward, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as markets price in the economic slowdown.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive forward returns during confirmed GDP contraction quarters, or the signal exhibits no directional accuracy above 50% in a 10-year backtest.
